title: Arachnis labrosa
intro: Arachnis labrosa, also known as the "star orchid," is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are said to help alleviate symptoms of fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Arachnis labrosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Arachnis labrosa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture, to take advantage of its reported health benefits.
taxonomy: Arachnis labrosa is a species of orchid, specifically within the genus Arachnis, which is a part of the family Orchidaceae. This genus is a small but unique group of flowering plants known for their showy, long-lasting blooms. The Arachnis labrosa is further classified into the subfamily Orchidoideae, which is a large and diverse group of orchids. The classification of Arachnis labrosa also includes the tribe Coelogyneae, which is a group of orchids characterized by their sympodial growth habits. The exact classification of Arachnis labrosa is further refined through its binomial nomenclature, Arachnis labrosa.

parts: Arachnis labrosa, also known as the 'Queen of the Night', is a species of orchid. Arachnis labrosa  is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia.
